observation.images.image.comment: Single external 3rd-person view (the only camera slot; Bridge has no wrist cam). float32 [0,1] CHW; the policy resizes to 224x224 internally (config resize_images_to), squashing the env's 480x640 aspect exactly like the starVLA eval client's cv.resize.

observation.text.comment: Task instruction, formatted into the Qwen3-VL prompt template; required by the VLA.

action.delta_eef_pos.comment: Bridge 'world_vector' eef translation delta in meters (5 Hz; per-step deltas ~7-10 mm std). OPEN: frame base vs world for SIMPLER/Bridge unverified — same flag as the widowx env contract.

action.delta_eef_rot.comment: Bridge 'rotation_delta' as extrinsic euler-XYZ (roll, pitch, yaw) rates — NOT axis-angle. The starVLA SimplerEnv client converts euler2axangle before env.step; the vla_jepa_widowx agent's adapter does the same (scipy as_rotvec). OPEN: frame=eef assumed, mirroring the widowx env contract's flag.

action.gripper.comment: Trained in {0,1} where 1=open (Bridge convention; stats above are that space, action.mask=0 excludes it from MIN_MAX). The lerobot postprocessor binarizes to 1-2*(g>0.5), i.e. {-1 open, +1 close} (robosuite sign). SimplerEnv WidowX expects the OPPOSITE sign (+1 open, -1 close — the official client emits 2*(open>0.5)-1), so the agent's adapter flips the sign before sending.

comment: VLA-JEPA (Qwen3-VL-2B + V-JEPA2 world model + DiT-B flow head; arXiv:2602.10098) finetuned for SimplerEnv visual-matching eval, WidowX 250s / Bridge embodiment. NO proprio: input_features is image-only (the checkpoint's normalizer carries vestigial observation.state stats, but the policy consumes no state at inference — the agent must NOT feed observation.state). Official eval protocol (starVLA model2simpler_interface.py, policy_setup=widowx_bridge): re-infer the 7-step chunk EVERY env step and adaptively ensemble overlapping chunks (AdaptiveEnsembler, horizon 7, alpha 0.1), then threshold the gripper — the agent reproduces this instead of LeRobot's pop-the-queue select_action. The paper also evaluates a Google-Robot SimplerEnv setup; this contract covers only the WidowX embodiment (one contract per embodiment; no Google-Robot env in this repo). OPEN: whether this single checkpoint serves both SimplerEnv embodiments (camera/action stats here are Bridge-consistent) — if a Google-Robot env is added, author a separate vla_jepa_google_robot.json with the sticky-gripper logic. Requires a lerobot build with policies/vla_jepa (git main, not 0.5.x).
